notes: Illustration briefs for GDD V2 sections requiring hero or schematic visuals. All currently rendered in-page as styled placeholders pending pipeline run. Aspect ratios are advisory; render layer may up-rez/crop to spec.

briefs.title: Lud-of-the-Mist border post at dusk
briefs.style_direction: Mirrlees-civic register rendered in oils — bourgeois town painting with one wrong color. Composition density medium-high. Palette dominated by accountancy browns and customs-house slate, with a single warm-rotten-honey accent on the contraband fruit at upper-right of the wagon.
briefs.composition: Three-quarter ground perspective. A horse-cart at center-left, half-loaded with lidded wooden crates. Customs officer at center-right, bent over a ledger on a podium; wearing the stolid coat of a Lud functionary. Carter at far left, half-turned away from the officer, eyes on the hill in the middle distance. The hill has the wrong color — that is the only fantastical element in the frame.
briefs.mood: Quiet civic discipline cracked by a single visible leak. The painting must feel like it could be in a town museum and is instead the evidence of a cosmological breach.
negative_prompts: no overt magic — no glow, no sparkle, no rune
no characters facing camera in heroic posture

briefs.title: 2:1:8 iceberg schematic
briefs.style_direction: Architectural diagram. Minimal. Two thin gold rules above one thinner blue rule above an enormous unlit dark mass with no contour. The mass occupies roughly two-thirds of the frame and is a felt presence rather than a shape.
briefs.composition: Vertical alignment. Top: a single thin gold line labeled 'surface (2)'. Below it: a thinner blue line labeled 'echo (1)'. Below it: a dark mass that fills the lower two-thirds of the frame, labeled small 'deep (8)' in a corner. Underneath, near the very bottom, a single italic phrase: 'one buried weight per Pane'.
briefs.mood: Stilled. The diagram should feel like a load-bearing engineering drawing, not an infographic.
negative_prompts: no actual iceberg illustration (no ice, no water)
no color beyond palette (gold, blue, dark)

briefs.title: Lampblack hierarchy — vertical stack
briefs.style_direction: Typographic diagram in the same restrained register. A vertical stack of seven terms, the top three rendered in gold at full strength, the bottom four progressively fading into the page. Hairline rules separate adjacent terms.
briefs.composition: Single vertical column. Terms in italic serif: Gesture / Cosmological-relation / Wound / Forbidden-act / Role / Silhouette / Prop. Top to bottom. Top three terms at full opacity in gold; bottom four step down 20-30 percent each, ending at near-illegible at 'Prop'.
briefs.mood: A hierarchy that reads as a moral one. The top is what carries the cosmology; the bottom is what survives only as residue.

briefs.title: Three pantheons, one table
briefs.style_direction: Three card-frames at the same wooden table, each frame rendered in a different visual grammar. The first frame is bordered with formal scribe-line in flat slate. The second frame is wrapped in a curling vine of names that escape the margin into the surrounding wood. The third frame is a window with no glass — the framing line is incomplete on the right side as if the rule could not finish drawing it. The figures inside the frames are abstracted — only their gestures legible — and none of them is looking at the others.
briefs.composition: Top-down view of a wooden table. Three card-shaped objects laid in a triangle. Each card's frame is in a different visual grammar. The lighting is even — no figure is foregrounded; the frames are the subject.
briefs.mood: Three pantheons sharing ground without sharing a court. The viewer should feel that the three frames cannot speak to each other.
negative_prompts: no pantheon symbols (no crosses, no thunderbolts, no ankh)

briefs.title: Six-test gate — abstract checkpoint
briefs.style_direction: Optional. A spread page of an in-world scholarly journal showing six adjacent stamps in the margin, four of them faded (the V1 inheritance) and two of them fresh (the V2 additions: Footnote, Prose-Signature). The journal page itself is not legible — the worldbuilding artifact is the marginalia.
briefs.composition: Page edge centered. Six rectangular stamp impressions vertically aligned in the margin. The top four are old, smudged, gold-faded. The bottom two are clean and recent, blue-ink crisp.
briefs.mood: A scholarly artifact. The reader should feel that the gate is a real procedure conducted on real pages.
negative_prompts: no readable text on the journal page

briefs.title: Eight-stage pipeline diagram
briefs.style_direction: Horizontal procedural diagram. Eight nodes in a row. Stages 2-5 shown in blue (stochastic block); stages 1, 6, 7, 8 shown in gold (deterministic frame). Connecting lines between nodes are thin. Above stage 7 floats a small iceberg-2:1:8 marker echoing the section 7 schematic.
briefs.composition: Eight nodes in a single horizontal sequence. Each node is a small numbered disc with a one-word label beneath: 1 grammar-lock / 2 figure / 3 axiom / 4 flavor / 5 art / 6 gate / 7 audit / 8 release. Stochastic block (2-5) shaded blue; deterministic block (1, 6, 7, 8) shaded gold. The 7-audit node has the iceberg marker hovering above it.
briefs.mood: Engineering drawing for a literary production line. Functional. Quiet.

briefs.title: Participation ladder — five rungs
briefs.style_direction: A vertical wooden ladder seen against a dark interior. Five rungs. The bottom rung is in shadow (Tier 0). The fifth rung carries a small gold plaque labelled 'Pane Record' (Tier 4). Between the second and third rung, a thin chain marked 'editorial gate'. Between the third and fourth rung, a heavier chain marked 'council gate'. The chains do not block the rungs — they hang beside them as procedural markers, not barriers.
briefs.composition: Single ladder centered, slightly to right of frame. Looking up the ladder from a low angle. The interior beyond the ladder is dim wood; no other figures.
briefs.mood: Architectural. A real ladder for real climbing, with the gates as procedural markers rather than locked doors.
negative_prompts: no glow on the top rung
no symbolic objects on the rungs
